title: Sidescan sonar imagery of widespread fossil and active cold seeps along the central Chilean continental margin
abstract: Sidescan sonar imagery from SONNE cruises is used to characterize widespread fossil and active cold seep sites along the central Chilean continental margin. The study documents the spatial extent and acoustic backscatter characteristics of cold seep fields, identifying both active gas escape features and older fossil seep structures. The distribution of cold seeps is linked to fluid migration pathways controlled by fault structures in the forearc.

location.description: Central Chilean continental margin between 35°S and 37°S; cold seep geomorphology and acoustics (JC23, March/April 2008 + SO210, Sep/Oct 2010)

seismic_lines.name: JC23 DTS-1 sidescan sonar + chirp subbottom survey — 35°–37°S Chile margin

data.name: SONNE Sidescan Sonar — Central Chile Margin Cold Seep Sites
data.description: Sidescan sonar imagery from SONNE cruises documenting widespread fossil and active cold seep sites along the central Chilean continental margin

data.name: SONNE Multibeam Bathymetry — Chile Margin Cold Seep Sites
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry from SONNE cruises mapping cold seep geomorphology (pockmarks, mounds) along the Chilean margin at multiple latitudes

data.name: SONNE Backscatter — Chile Margin Cold Seep Sites
data.description: Backscatter intensity data from multibeam sonar used to detect acoustic anomalies at cold seep sites along the Chilean margin

data.name: Ocean bottom seismometer and ocean bottom hydrophone sgy-files of seismic refraction and wide angle data from profile P03 from James Cook cruise JC23 with links to data files

_data_note: Geo-Marine Letters paper; behind paywall. Multiple SONNE cruises; specific cruise IDs not confirmed. Repository uncertain.

processing.summary: Multibeam bathymetry and PARASOUND subbottom profiler data from SONNE cruises along the Chilean margin are analyzed to characterize cold seep geomorphology and acoustic signatures. Gas escape structures, pockmarks, and carbonate mounds are identified and correlated with fluid migration pathways and forearc fault structures.
software: PRISM (NOC Southampton, Le Bas et al. 1995)
processing.notes: Primary cruise JC23 of RRS James Cook (March/April 2008): Simrad EM120 multibeam, DTS-1 deep-towed sidescan 75 kHz, chirp subbottom 2–10 kHz, 105 cu in GI-gun + 4-ch mini-streamer. Ground-truth cruise SO210 of RV Sonne (Sep/Oct 2010): OFOS video sled. Existing bathymetry also from R/V Sonne, R/V Meteor, R/V Vidal Gormaz.

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. CRITICAL: authors completely wrong [Petersen, Bowden] → [Linke, Peter; Kläschen, Dirk; Bialas, Jörg] (prior JSON used authors from a different Klaucke 2010 New Zealand paper). vessel: 'R/V SONNE' → ['RRS James Cook (JC23, March/April 2008)', 'R/V SONNE (SO210, Sep/Oct 2010)']; year_acquired: 2007 → 2008; source_volume_cui: null → 105 (GI-gun); channel_count: null → 4 (4-ch mini-streamer); bounding_box restricted from 33–46°S to 35–37°S; software: CARIS HIPS/SIPS → Caraibes (IFREMER) + PRISM (NOC Southampton). Study area is 35°S–37°S offshore Concepcion. No data repository URLs found in paper.
